summary: Ported the entire project-memory MCP server from Python to Go, adopting a Pure Go MCP gateway with dual transport support (stdio default, --http for dev). The new architecture follows SOLID/DRY principles with segregated Reader/Writer/Searcher interfaces, organized across 10 packages (domain, config, storage, server, inspector, git, llm, build). Uses modernc.org/sqlite for pure Go SQLite without CGO dependencies. Implements 5 MCP tools plus a briefing prompt via the mcp-go SDK, with a 6-phase build pipeline: discover, batch, extract, synthesize, score. Includes an OpenRouter LLM client with rate limiting and retry logic, achieving 70.5% statement coverage with 129 tests.
